critic_raw:
StyleSpace attributes discovered as explanatory on face datasets are correlated with demographic characteristics (apparent race, gender, age). When these attributes are presented as 'explaining the classifier's decision,' they may be encoding protected characteristics rather than task-relevant features. A replication study that accepts visually interpretable attributes as validation of explanation quality without auditing for demographic encoding is measuring GAN quality, not explanation faithfulness.

Counterfactual explanation quality is evaluated through classifier response change (does editing the attribute change the predicted class?) and visual quality. This conflates two distinct properties: faithfulness (does the attribute causally drive the classifier?) and disentanglement (does editing the attribute change only that attribute?). A classifier may change its output because an unintended correlated feature changed, not because the intended explanatory attribute changed.

StyleGAN2's StyleSpace disentanglement property was characterized specifically for the face domain and FFHQ-trained models. Applying the same explanation methodology to non-face classifiers (or using a different StyleGAN2 checkpoint) requires re-establishing that StyleSpace remains disentangled in the new domain. This is not addressed in the task description.
all_issues_raised: StyleSpace attributes on face datasets correlate with protected demographic characteristics; accepting visually interpretable attributes as explanation validation without demographic auditing conflates GAN quality with explanation faithfulness.

Explanation quality evaluation conflates faithfulness (the attribute causally drives the classifier) with disentanglement (editing the attribute doesn't change correlated features); a correlated unintended feature could be driving the classifier change.
StyleSpace disentanglement was characterized specifically for FFHQ-trained StyleGAN2; applying the explanation method to other domains requires re-establishing this property, which is not addressed.
all_issues_adjudicated: StyleSpace attributes on face datasets correlate with protected demographic characteristics; accepting visually interpretable attributes as explanation validation without demographic auditing conflates GAN quality with explanation faithfulness.

Explanation quality evaluation conflates faithfulness (the attribute causally drives the classifier) with disentanglement (editing the attribute doesn't change correlated features); a correlated unintended feature could be driving the classifier change.
StyleSpace disentanglement was characterized specifically for FFHQ-trained StyleGAN2; applying the explanation method to other domains requires re-establishing this property, which is not addressed.
